channel.title: Identity on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Identity on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: ai: Building Agents that Act on Your Behalf with Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description:
Toolboxes in Microsoft Foundry are presented as a way to let agents act on a user’s behalf while handling delegated identity across tools and services. The post highlights using a private Entra-protected MCP server alongside Microsoft’s managed Work IQ MCP server in the same agent flow.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a post on applying least-privilege principles to AI agents, focusing on identity, access control, tool binding, and auditing as agents become more autonomous.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: ​​What’s new in Microsoft Security: June 2026
item.description:
Microsoft’s June 2026 security update post says the month’s changes focus on identity and multicloud foundations, data protection across locations, and securing developer workflows for AI-related work. The provided excerpt does not include product-level details or specific feature announcements.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Beyond the benchmark: Advancing security at AI speed
item.description:
Microsoft Security says it has advanced its agentic vulnerability detection system, MDASH, and integrated it into workflows across Windows, Azure, and identity systems. The post focuses on applying the system in real-world security operations rather than benchmark results.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: AI is accelerating cyberattacks—here’s how to stay ahead
item.description:
Microsoft describes how its identity and security products use unified signals to help teams prevent, detect, and respond to AI-accelerated attacks faster. The post is a security-focused overview rather than a feature release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Entra server principals on Azure SQL Database
item.description:
Microsoft Entra server principals (logins) for Azure SQL Database are now generally available. You can now create logins with CREATE LOGIN ... FROM EXTERNAL PROVIDER in the virtual master database, aligning Microsoft Entra identities with SQL logins.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Microsoft Security success stories: How St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are securing AI foundations
item.description:
Microsoft highlights how St. Luke’s and ManpowerGroup are using governance, identity, and cloud security controls to support AI adoption at scale. The post frames security as part of the foundation for AI deployment rather than a separate concern.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Entra-only identities with Azure Files
item.description:
Azure Files now supports generally available Entra-only identities for SMB access. This lets organizations use cloud-native identities to access file shares without requiring Active Directory or hybrid identity infrastructure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Defense in depth for autonomous AI agents
item.description:
Microsoft says autonomous AI agents require updated defense-in-depth controls centered on application-layer design, identity, and human oversight. The post frames agent security as an evolution of existing defense-in-depth practices rather than a new model.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog
